messages.content: Build a retrying HTTP fetch effect in Effect v3 using exponential backoff with jitter and a cap on total retries.
What's the Effect v3 way to retry an effect with exponential backoff capped at 10 seconds total? Show a minimal example.
Implement a retrying database query with strict types: typed error, composed exponential+jitter schedule bounded by recurs(3).
Explain briefly how Schedule composition works in Effect v3, then show a typed retry policy bounded by both attempt count and total duration.
Write a retrying S3 upload in Effect v3. Constraints: tagged error, exponential backoff, at most 7 retries, log each failure using Effect.tapError.
Build an email-send effect that retries on SmtpError with exponential+jitter backoff bounded to 4 attempts total.
How do I retry an Effect v3 effect for up to 2 seconds of wall time, no attempt cap? Show a minimal typed example.
Explain when to use Schedule.recurs vs Schedule.upTo in Effect v3, then show both combined with exponential backoff.
Implement a retrying JSON-API call in Effect v3 with strict types and exponential+jitter backoff bounded by recurs(3).

Add jitter to this exponential-backoff retry: ```ts
Effect.retry(eff, Schedule.exponential('100 millis'))
```
Build a reusable retry helper in Effect v3 that wraps any Effect with jittered exponential backoff bounded by N attempts.
Write a retrying RPC call. Constraints: strict types, no jitter, exponential base, hard stop after 2 retries.
What's the idiomatic Effect v3 way to log each retry attempt while retrying? Show a minimal typed example.

Add a 20-second wall-time cap to this retry: ```ts
Effect.retry(eff, Schedule.intersect(Schedule.exponential('100 millis'), Schedule.recurs(10)))
```
Build an effect that retries forever with jittered exponential backoff but will always stop after 1 minute of total wall time.
Refactor the idea of a while-loop of three tries into Effect v3 using Schedule retry with exponential backoff.
Implement a retrying Redis GET in Effect v3 with a typed error and jittered exponential backoff bounded by recurs(4).
Write a retrying effect in Effect v3 that uses exponential backoff and stops at whichever comes first: 5 retries OR 3 seconds wall time.
Build a program in Effect v3 that runs three sequential steps with Effect.all and returns the typed tuple.
Build a program in Effect v3 that runs three HTTP fetches fully in parallel using Effect.all with unbounded concurrency.
What's the Effect v3 way to run 10 effects at most 3 at a time? Show a minimal typed example.
What's the Effect v3 way to run effects and get back a named struct instead of a tuple? Show a minimal typed example.
Explain briefly how Effect.all's return type depends on the input shape in Effect v3, then show one tuple and one struct example.
Implement parallel aggregation of user, posts, and followers effects in Effect v3 with strict types and concurrency: 'unbounded'.

Convert this sequential Effect.all to run with a concurrency limit of 4: ```ts
Effect.all(effects)
```
Refactor these chained awaits into Effect v3 using Effect.all with unbounded concurrency: imagine three independent fetches (user, orders, preferences).
Write an Effect v3 program that validates three independent fields in parallel. Constraints: object input to Effect.all, bounded concurrency of 3.
Build an Effect v3 program inside Effect.gen that uses Effect.all with concurrency: 'unbounded' to fan out N requests.
What does `concurrency: 'inherit'` do on Effect.all in Effect v3? Show a minimal typed example.
Implement an Effect v3 program that runs two independent effects in parallel and destructures the typed tuple inside Effect.gen.
Build an Effect v3 program inside Effect.gen that uses Effect.all with an object argument and destructures the struct result.

Increase this Effect.all from concurrency 2 to unbounded: ```ts
Effect.all(effects, { concurrency: 2 })
```
Write an Effect v3 program that uses Effect.all with concurrency 5 and short-circuits on the first error. Include a tagged error.

Convert this `Promise.all` to Effect v3's Effect.all (unbounded) with tagged errors: ```ts
Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB(), fetchC()])
```
Build an Effect v3 program with two phases: fan out 4 fetches in parallel, then call a summariser on the array result.
Implement an Effect v3 parallel load with struct input and a union error type in strict mode.
When does Effect.all run sequentially vs in parallel? Show a minimal typed example of the default behaviour.
Write an Effect v3 function that maps an array of IDs to fetch effects and combines them. Constraints: bounded concurrency of 4, typed tagged error.
